subject: [PATCH v4 10/10] docs: fuse: add io-uring buffer pool and zero-copy documentation

2026-06-12.patch_summary: This patch adds documentation to the Linux kernel's FUSE (Filesystem in Userspace) module, specifically covering its usage of io-uring buffer pools and zero-copy techniques. The new documentation explains how buffer pools decouple the relationship between entries and payload buffers, allowing for more efficient memory usage and enabling optimizations such as incremental buffer consumption. It also describes how to use this feature by setting a flag during registration and providing the payload buffer pool through an iovec payload in the first submission queue element (sqe). The documentation includes a high-level overview of how fuse manages the buffer pool internally, including how buffers are selected, copied, and recycled.
